ucs.title: [LLM] TA459 (Suspected China) weaponized RTF/Office doc → script interpreter — ZeroT/NetTraveler delivery chain
ucs.description: Hunts TA459's signature initial-access chain: a spearphishing attachment (RTF/DOC) opened in Word/Excel/PowerPoint or Equation Editor (EQNEDT32.EXE — abused via CVE-2017-11882/CVE-2017-0199) that spawns a script interpreter (PowerShell, wscript/cscript, mshta, cmd) used to stage the ZeroT loader and ultimately deploy NetTraveler or PlugX. This is the exact T1566.001 → T1203 → T1204.002 → T1059.001/005 sequence Proofpoint documented for TA459 against Russian, Mongolian, and Belarusian targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_hash) as child_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("WINWORD.EXE","EXCEL.EXE","POWERPNT.EXE","OUTLOOK.EXE","EQNEDT32.EXE") AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe")) AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where (match(cmdline,"(?i)(EncodedCommand|-enc |IEX|Invoke-Expression|DownloadString|FromBase64String|hidden|bypass|http(s)?:\/\/)") OR parent_process_name="EQNEDT32.EXE") | rename dest as host, user as user_name | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// TA459 — Office/EQNEDT32 spawning script interpreter (ZeroT/PlugX delivery)
let officeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
let scriptChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (officeParents)
| where FileName in~ (scriptChildren)
// EQNEDT32 spawning ANYTHING is enough — it should never spawn a child
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "eqnedt32.exe"
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any (
        "-EncodedCommand","-enc ","-ec ","IEX","Invoke-Expression",
        "DownloadString","DownloadFile","FromBase64String",
        "-w hidden","-windowstyle hidden","-nop","-noprofile",
        "http://","https://","bitsadmin /transfer","certutil -urlcache")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent      = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          Child       = FileName,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256,
          IsRemoteSession = InitiatingProcessTokenElevation
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// TA459 — Office/EQNEDT32 spawning script interpreter (Sentinel SecurityEvent / 4688)
let officeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
let scriptChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User" and Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
         ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf in (officeParents)
| where ChildLeaf  in (scriptChildren)
| where ParentLeaf == "eqnedt32.exe"
   or CommandLine has_any (
        "-EncodedCommand","-enc ","-ec ","IEX","Invoke-Expression",
        "DownloadString","DownloadFile","FromBase64String",
        "-w hidden","-windowstyle hidden","-nop","-noprofile",
        "http://","https://","bitsadmin /transfer","certutil -urlcache")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentLeaf, ParentProcessName,
          ChildLeaf,  NewProcessName,
          CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: TA459 Office or Equation Editor Spawning Script Interpreter (ZeroT/PlugX/NetTraveler Delivery)
id: 7f3b1c4d-9a82-4f51-87e1-0f5b1c6c9a2e
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects TA459's documented initial-access chain — a weaponized Office
  document (RTF/DOC) opened in Word/Excel/PowerPoint or the Equation
  Editor (EQNEDT32.EXE, abused via CVE-2017-11882 / CVE-2017-0199)
  spawning a script interpreter (PowerShell, wscript/cscript, mshta,
  cmd, rundll32, regsvr32, certutil, bitsadmin) — the exact loader-stage
  pattern used to drop the ZeroT downloader and follow-on NetTraveler /
  PlugX implants.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0062/
  - https://www.proofpoint.com/us/threat-insight/post/apt-targets-russia-belarus-zerot-plugx
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.t1203
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.005
  - attack.g0062
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent_office:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\WINWORD.EXE'
      - '\EXCEL.EXE'
      - '\POWERPNT.EXE'
      - '\OUTLOOK.EXE'
      - '\EQNEDT32.EXE'
  child_interpreter:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
  parent_eqnedt:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\EQNEDT32.EXE'
  suspicious_cmdline:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '-EncodedCommand'
      - '-enc '
      - 'IEX'
      - 'Invoke-Expression'
      - 'DownloadString'
      - 'DownloadFile'
      - 'FromBase64String'
      - '-w hidden'
      - '-windowstyle hidden'
      - '-nop'
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
      - 'bitsadmin /transfer'
      - 'certutil -urlcache'
  filter_machine_account:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: parent_office and child_interpreter and (parent_eqnedt or suspicious_cmdline) and not filter_machine_account
fields:
  - ComputerName
  - User
  - ParentImage
  - Image
  - CommandLine
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate Office automation (Power Automate Desktop, custom macros) launching cmd/PowerShell — baseline-allowlist by signing/account
  - Admin troubleshooting via macro-enabled diagnostic workbooks
level: high
ucs.rationale: Proofpoint's reporting on TA459 (the cluster MITRE tracks as G0062) specifically describes the actor weaponizing RTF and DOC attachments that exploit Office Equation Editor (CVE-2017-11882) and OLE handlers (CVE-2017-0199) to drop the ZeroT downloader, which then stages NetTraveler and PlugX. EQNEDT32.EXE has no legitimate reason to spawn a child process, making any child a near-zero-FP signal; co

ucs.title: [LLM] TA459 PlugX/NetTraveler DLL side-loading via signed binary in user-writable path
ucs.description: Hunts TA459's hallmark second-stage installation pattern: a legitimately-signed executable (commonly renamed AV/security/messenger binaries) dropped into %TEMP% / %APPDATA% / %ProgramData% that side-loads an attacker-supplied DLL from the same directory — the canonical PlugX and NetTraveler persistence technique observed across TA459 campaigns against Eurasian government and dissident targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as exe_path values(Filesystem.file_hash) as exe_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.exe" AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | join type=inner dest process_guid [| tstats summariesonly=true count values(Filesystem.file_path) as dll_path values(Filesystem.file_hash) as dll_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_guid Filesystem.file_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename file_name as dll_name] | rename dest as host | sort - lastTime | table firstTime lastTime host file_name exe_path exe_hash dll_name dll_path dll_hash
ucs.kql:
// TA459 — PlugX/NetTraveler DLL side-loading from user-writable path
let writablePaths = dynamic([
    @"\appdata\local\temp\",
    @"\appdata\roaming\",
    @"\programdata\",
    @"\users\public\"
]);
// 1. Catch DLLs loaded from suspicious paths by signed-looking binaries
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| extend FolderPathLower = tolower(FolderPath),
         InitFolderLower = tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
| where FolderPathLower has_any (writablePaths)
// DLL is in same dir as the loader (classic side-load)
| where tostring(split(FolderPathLower, "\\")[-2]) == tostring(split(InitFolderLower, "\\")[-2])
   and FolderPathLower != @"c:\windows\system32\"
// Loader is NOT in System32/SysWOW64/Program Files — already in user-writable path
| where InitFolderLower has_any (writablePaths)
// Exclude common installer/uninstaller noise
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("msiexec.exe","setup.exe","installer.exe","update.exe","squirrel.exe")
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          Loader      = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          LoaderPath  = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          LoaderSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          LoadedDLL   = FileName,
          LoadedPath  = FolderPath,
          LoadedSHA256 = SHA256,
          LoaderCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| join kind=leftanti (
    // 30-day baseline of (loader,DLL) pairs seen org-wide — drops legit installers
    DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp between (ago(30d) .. ago(1d))
    | summarize Hosts = dcount(DeviceId) by InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName
    | where Hosts > 5
    | project Loader = InitiatingProcessFileName, LoadedDLL = FileName
) on Loader, LoadedDLL
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// TA459 — DLL side-loading from user-writable path (ASIM ImFileEvent + ImProcessCreate)
let writablePaths = dynamic([
    @"\appdata\local\temp\",
    @"\appdata\roaming\",
    @"\programdata\",
    @"\users\public\"
]);
let WindowMin = 10m;
// 1. EXE drops in suspicious path
let droppedExe = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".exe"
    | extend p = tolower(TargetFilePath)
    | where p has_any (writablePaths)
    | project ExeTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ExeName = TargetFileName,
              ExePath = TargetFilePath, ExeSHA256 = TargetFileSHA256;
// 2. DLL drops in same dir within 10 min — classic side-load staging
let droppedDll = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".dll"
    | extend p = tolower(TargetFilePath)
    | where p has_any (writablePaths)
    | project DllTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, DllName = TargetFileName,
              DllPath = TargetFilePath, DllSHA256 = TargetFileSHA256;
droppedExe
| join kind=inner droppedDll on DvcHostname
| extend ExeDir = tolower(strcat_array(array_slice(split(ExePath,"\\"),0,-2),"\\")),
         DllDir = tolower(strcat_array(array_slice(split(DllPath,"\\"),0,-2),"\\"))
| where ExeDir == DllDir
| where DllTime between (ExeTime .. ExeTime + WindowMin)
| project DvcHostname, ExeTime, ExeName, ExePath, ExeSHA256,
          DllTime, DllName, DllPath, DllSHA256
| order by ExeTime desc
ucs.rationale: Both PlugX and NetTraveler — TA459's signature implants per Proofpoint and Kaspersky reporting — install via DLL side-loading: a legitimately-signed binary (often a renamed AV component, McAfee mc-fw helpers, NVIDIA, or messenger executables) is dropped to %TEMP%/%APPDATA% alongside a malicious DLL whose name matches a real import. This UC catches the staging step (exe + dll co-located in user-wri
